def solve(nums, queries)
  # Precompute popcount-depth for each number in nums
  # Since k can be up to 5, we can precompute the depth for each number
  # and store it in an array.
  depths = nums.map { |num| compute_depth(num) }

  # For efficient range queries and updates, we use a segment tree
  # with each node storing a frequency array of depths (0 to 5)
  # Since k is small (0 to 5), this is feasible.
  segment_tree = SegmentTree.new(depths.length) do |left, right|
    freq = Array.new(6, 0)
    (left..right).each { |i| freq[depths[i]] += 1 }
    freq
  end

  answer = []

  queries.each do |query|
    if query[0] == 1
      l, r, k = query[1], query[2], query[3]
      result = segment_tree.query(l, r)[k]
      answer << result
    else
      idx, val = query[1], query[2]
      old_val = nums[idx]
      new_depth = compute_depth(val)
      nums[idx] = val
      segment_tree.update(idx, new_depth)
    end
  end

  answer
end

class SegmentTree
  attr_accessor :size, :tree, :lazy

  def initialize(n, &build_block)
    @size = n
    @tree = Array.new(4 * n, Array.new(6, 0))
    @lazy = Array.new(4 * n, Array.new(6, 0))
    build_block.call(0, n - 1)
  end

  def build(left, right, node = 1)
    if left == right
      @tree[node] = Array.new(6, 0)
      @tree[node][depths[left]] += 1
    else
      mid = (left + right) / 2
      build(left, mid, 2 * node)
      build(mid + 1, right, 2 * node + 1)
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end
  end

  def update(pos, value, node = 1, left = 0, right = nil)
    right ||= @size - 1
    if left == right
      @tree[node][value] = 1
      @tree[node][old_value] = 0
      old_value = depths[left]
    else
      mid = (left + right) / 2
      if pos <= mid
        update(pos, value, 2 * node, left, mid)
      else
        update(pos, value, 2 * node + 1, mid + 1, right)
      end
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end
  end

  def query(l, r, node = 1, left = 0, right = nil)
    right ||= @size - 1
    if r < left || right < l
      return Array.new(6, 0)
    end
    if l <= left && right <= r
      return @tree[node].clone
    end
    mid = (left + right) / 2
    left_result = query(l, r, 2 * node, left, mid)
    right_result = query(l, r, 2 * node + 1, mid + 1, right)
    left_result + right_result
  end

  private

  def compute_depth(num)
    depth = 0
    while num > 0
      num = num.bit_count
      depth += 1
    end
    depth
  end
end

-include_lib("eunit/include/eunit.hrl").

-spec solve(list(integer()), list(list(integer()))) -> list(integer()).

solve(nums, Queries) ->
    Depths = compute_depths(nums),
    SegmentTree = build_segment_tree(length(nums), Depths),
    process_queries(Queries, nums, Depths, SegmentTree).

compute_depths(nums) ->
    lists:map(fun(N) -> compute_depth(N) end, nums).

compute_depth(N) ->
    Depth = 0,
    Num = N,
    while Num > 0,
    Num = bit_count(Num),
    Depth = Depth + 1
    end,
    Depth.

bit_count(N) ->
    erlang:bit_count(N).

build_segment_tree(Size, Depths) ->
    Tree = build_tree(1, 0, Size - 1, Depths),
    {Tree, []}.

build_tree(Node, Left, Right, Depths) ->
    if
        Left == Right ->
            Tree = make_freq(Depths[Left]),
            {Tree, []};
        true ->
            Mid = (Left + Right) div 2,
            LeftTree = build_tree(2*Node, Left, Mid, Depths),
            RightTree = build_tree(2*Node+1, Mid+1, Right, Depths),
            Tree = merge_trees(LeftTree, RightTree),
            {Tree, []}
    end.

make_freq(Depth) ->
    Freq = lists:foldl(
        fun(D, Acc) ->
            Freq = lists:foldl(
                fun(_D, Acc2) ->
                    Acc2
                end,
                lists:map(fun(_) -> 0 end, lists:seq(0,5)),
                Acc
            ),
            Freq
        end,
        lists:map(fun(_) -> 0 end, lists:seq(0,5)),
        [Depth]
    ),
    Freq.

merge_trees(LeftTree, RightTree) ->
    lists:zip_with(fun(A, B) -> A + B end, LeftTree, RightTree).

process_queries(Queries, nums, Depths, SegmentTree) ->
    process_queries(Queries, nums, Depths, SegmentTree, []).

process_queries([], _, _, _, Acc) ->
    lists:reverse(Acc).

process_queries([Q | Qs], nums, Depths, SegmentTree, Acc) ->
    case hd(Q) of
        1 ->
            [_, L, R, K] = Q,
            Result = query_segment_tree(SegmentTree, L, R, K),
            process_queries(Qs, nums, Depths, SegmentTree, [Result | Acc]);
        2 ->
            [_,Idx,Val] = Q,
            OldVal = nums[Idx],
            NewDepth = compute_depth(Val),
            NewNums = update_num(nums, Idx, Val),
            NewDepths = update_depths(Depths, Idx, NewDepth),
            NewSegmentTree = update_segment_tree(SegmentTree, Idx, NewDepth),
            process_queries(Qs, NewNums, NewDepths, NewSegmentTree, Acc)
    end.

query_segment_tree(SegmentTree, L, R, K) ->
    {Tree, _} = query_range(SegmentTree, 1, 0, length(SegmentTree) - 1, L, R),
    Tree[K].

update_segment_tree(SegmentTree, Pos, Value) ->
    update_range(SegmentTree, 1, 0, length(SegmentTree) - 1, Pos, Value).

update_num(nums, Idx, Val) ->
    lists:sublist(nums, Idx-1) ++ [Val] ++ lists:sublist(nums, Idx+1).

update_depths(Depths, Idx, NewDepth) ->
    lists:sublist(Depths, Idx-1) ++ [NewDepth] ++ lists:sublist(Depths, Idx+1).

query_range(SegmentTree, Node, Left, Right, L, R) ->
    case Left > Right of
        true ->
            {[], []};
        false ->
            case L > Right orelse R < Left of
                true ->
                    {[], []};
                false ->
                    if
                        Left == Right ->
                            {Tree, _} = SegmentTree,
                            {Tree, []};
                        true ->
                            Mid = (Left + Right) div 2,
                            LeftTree = query_range(SegmentTree, 2*Node, Left, Mid, L, R),
                            RightTree = query_range(SegmentTree, 2*Node+1, Mid+1, Right, L, R),
                            {merge_trees(LeftTree, RightTree), []}
                    end
            end
    end.

update_range(SegmentTree, Node, Left, Right, Pos, Value) ->
    case Left == Right of
        true ->
            {Tree, _} = SegmentTree,
            NewTree = update_freq(Tree, Value),
            {NewTree, []};
        false ->
            Mid = (Left + Right) div 2,
            if
                Pos <= Mid ->
                    NewLeftTree = update_range(SegmentTree, 2*Node, Left, Mid, Pos, Value),
                    NewRightTree = query_range(SegmentTree, 2*Node+1, Mid+1, Right, Left, Right),
                    {merge_trees(NewLeftTree, NewRightTree), []};
                true ->
                    NewRightTree = update_range(SegmentTree, 2*Node+1, Mid+1, Right, Pos, Value),
                    NewLeftTree = query_range(SegmentTree, 2*Node, Left, Mid, Left, Right),
                    {merge_trees(NewLeftTree, NewRightTree), []}
            end
    end.

update_freq(Freq, Value) ->
    lists:map(
        fun(I) ->
            if
                I == Value ->
                    1;
                true ->
                    0
            end
        end,
        lists:seq(0,5)
    ).

def solve(nums, queries)
  # Precompute popcount-depth for each number in nums
  # Since k can be up to 5, we can precompute the depth for each number
  # and store it in an array.
  depths = nums.map { |num| compute_depth(num) }

  # For efficient range queries and updates, we use a segment tree
  # with each node storing a frequency array of depths (0 to 5)
  # Since k is small (0 to 5), this is feasible.
  segment_tree = SegmentTree.new(depths.length) do |left, right|
    freq = Array.new(6, 0)
    (left..right).each { |i| freq[depths[i]] += 1 }
    freq
  end

  answer = []

  queries.each do |query|
    if query[0] == 1
      l, r, k = query[1], query[2], query[3]
      result = segment_tree.query(l, r)[k]
      answer << result
    else
      idx, val = query[1], query[2]
      old_val = nums[idx]
      new_depth = compute_depth(val)
      nums[idx] = val
      segment_tree.update(idx, new_depth)
    end
  end

  answer
end

class SegmentTree
  attr_accessor :size, :tree, :lazy

  def initialize(n, &build_block)
    @size = n
    @tree = Array.new(4 * n, Array.new(6, 0))
    @lazy = Array.new(4 * n, Array.new(6, 0))
    build_block.call(0, n - 1)
  end

  def build(left, right, node = 1)
    if left == right
      @tree[node] = Array.new(6, 0)
      @tree[node][depths[left]] += 1
    else
      mid = (left + right) / 2
      build(left, mid, 2 * node)
      build(mid + 1, right, 2 * node + 1)
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end
  end

  def update(pos, value, node = 1, left = 0, right = nil)
    right ||= @size - 1
    if left == right
      @tree[node][value] = 1
      @tree[node][old_value] = 0
      old_value = depths[left]
    else
      mid = (left + right) / 2
      if pos <= mid
        update(pos, value, 2 * node, left, mid)
      else
        update(pos, value, 2 * node + 1, mid + 1, right)
      end
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end
  end

  def query(l, r, node = 1, left = 0, right = nil)
    right ||= @size - 1
    if r < left || right < l
      return Array.new(6, 0)
    end
    if l <= left && right <= r
      return @tree[node].clone
    end
    mid = (left + right) / 2
    left_result = query(l, r, 2 * node, left, mid)
    right_result = query(l, r, 2 * node + 1, mid + 1, right)
    left_result + right_result
  end

  private

  def compute_depth(num)
    depth = 0
    while num > 0
      num = num.bit_count
      depth += 1
    end
    depth
  end
end

def solve(nums, queries)
  # Precompute popcount-depth for each number in nums
  # Since k can be up to 5, we can precompute the depth for each number
  # and store it in an array.
  depths = nums.map { |num| compute_depth(num) }

  # For efficient range queries and updates, we use a segment tree
  # with each node storing a frequency array of depths (0 to 5)
  # Since k is small (0 to 5), this is feasible.
  segment_tree = SegmentTree.new(depths.length) do |left, right|
    freq = Array.new(6, 0)
    (left..right).each { |i| freq[depths[i]] += 1 }
    freq
  end

  answer = []

  queries.each do |query|
    if query[0] == 1
      l, r, k = query[1], query[2], query[3]
      result = segment_tree.query(l, r)[k]
      answer << result
    else
      idx, val = query[1], query[2]
      old_val = nums[idx]
      new_depth = compute_depth(val)
      nums[idx] = val
      segment_tree.update(idx, new_depth)
    end
  end

  answer
end

class SegmentTree
  attr_accessor :size, :tree, :lazy

  def initialize(n, &build_block)
    @size = n
    @tree = Array.new(4 * n, Array.new(6, 0))
    @lazy = Array.new(4 * n, Array.new(6, 0))
    build_block.call(0, n - 1)
  end

  def build(left, right, node = 1)
    if left == right
      @tree[node] = Array.new(6, 0)
      @tree[node][depths[left]] += 1
    else
      mid = (left + right) / 2
      build(left, mid, 2 * node)

class SegmentTree
  attr_accessor :size, :tree, :lazy

  def initialize(n, &build_block)
    @size = n

    build_block.call(0, n - 1)

class SegmentTree
  attr_accessor :size, :tree, :lazy

  def initialize(n, &build_block)
    @size = n
    @tree = Array.new(4 * n, Array.new(6, 0))
    @lazy = Array.new(4 * n, Array.new(6, 0))
    build_block.call(0, n - 1)

      @tree[node] = Array.new(6, 0)

      build(left, mid, 2 * node)

      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end
  end

    end

def solve(nums, queries)
  # Precompute popcount-depth for each number in nums
  # Since k can be up to 5, we can precompute the depth for each number
  # and store it in an array.
  depths = nums.map { |num| compute_depth(num) }

  # For efficient range queries and updates, we use a segment tree
  # with each node storing a frequency array of depths (0 to 5)
  # Since k is small (0 to 5), this is feasible.
  segment_tree = SegmentTree.new(depths.length) do |left, right|
    freq = Array.new(6, 0)
    (left..right).each { |i| freq[depths[i]] += 1 }
    freq
  end

  answer = []

  queries.each do |query|
    if query[0] == 1
      l, r, k = query[1], query[2], query[3]
      result = segment_tree.query(l, r)[k]
      answer << result
    else
      idx, val = query[1], query[2]
      old_val = nums[idx]
      new_depth = compute_depth(val)
      nums[idx] = val
      segment_tree.update(idx, new_depth)
    end
  end

  answer
end

class SegmentTree
  attr_accessor :size, :tree, :lazy

  def initialize(n, &build_block)
    @size = n
    @tree = Array.new(4 * n, Array.new(6, 0))
    @lazy = Array.new(4 * n, Array.new(6, 0))
    build_block.call(0, n - 1)
  end

  def build(left, right, node = 1)
    if left == right
      @tree[node] = Array.new(6, 0)
      @tree[node][depths[left]] += 1
    else
      mid = (left + right) / 2
      build(left, mid, 2 * node)
      build(mid + 1, right, 2 * node + 1)
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end
  end

  def update(pos, value, node = 1, left = 0, right = nil)
    right ||= @size - 1
    if left == right
      @tree[node][value] = 1
      @tree[node][old_value] = 0
      old_value = depths[left]
    else
      mid = (left + right) / 2
      if pos <= mid
        update(pos, value, 2 * node, left, mid)
      else
        update(pos, value, 2 * node + 1, mid + 1, right)
      end
      @tree[node] = @tree[2 * node] + @tree[2 * node + 1]
    end
  end

  def query(l, r, node = 1, left = 0, right = nil)
    right ||= @size - 1
    if r < left || right < l
      return Array.new(6, 0)
    end
    if l <= left && right <= r
      return @tree[node].clone
    end
    mid = (left + right) / 2
    left_result = query(l, r, 2 * node, left, mid)
    right_result = query(l, r, 2 * node + 1, mid + 1, right)
    left_result + right_result
  end

  private

  def compute_depth(num)
    depth = 0
    while num > 0
      num = num.bit_count
      depth += 1
    end
    depth
  end
end
